A taxpayer ID or Employer Identification Number (EIN) is a unique identifier the IRS gives to U.S. companies for tax reporting. This number is nine digits in length. The first two digits are alpha-numeric. You may include or exclude the dash when entering your EIN in this field.
Examples:
12-3456789 or TF-3456789
123456789 or TF3456789
StandardQuestions.GRLabel: Taxpayer ID or Employer Identification Number (EIN)

CustomQuestions.HelpText: In New York, companies that sell clothing or footwear are required to file Schedule H forms. Check this box to file Schedule H.

CustomQuestions.Question: New York Sales Tax Identification Number
CustomQuestions.HelpText:
Your state registration ID is most likely the same as your Federal Employee ID Number (FEIN). You're assigned a New York Sales Tax ID Number only if you don't already have a FEIN.

Find your temporary New York ID number in the documents you received when you registered your business to collect and remit sales and use tax in New York. This number will be used to identify your returns in New York until you get a FEIN.
Your ID will be 9 digits OR will begin with TF or NY + 7 digits OR 9 digits + suffix C, SC, SS, S1, S2 or any number ranging from 01-99. Examples: 123456789, TF1234567, 123456789SC or 12345678922

CustomQuestions.Question: Do you have retail sales of vapor products in New York?
CustomQuestions.HelpText: In New York there is a 20% supplemental sales tax on retail sales of vapor products. The tax is collected by vapor products dealers. Any business that intends to sell vapor products must be registered as a vapor products dealer before making sales of vapor products.

CustomQuestions.Question: Ohio UST 1 Vendor's License Number
CustomQuestions.HelpText:
Find your vendor's license number by checking the documentation you received when you registered your business for a vendor’s license in Ohio. A taxpayer cannot file a sales or use tax return using their Social Security number or FEIN. Vendor’s license numbers that have a prefix of 95 or 96 are not sales or use tax accounts.

If your vendor's license number is one of the following, you should file the UST 1 return for sales tax:
County vendor’s licenses – prefix 01 thru 88
Master cumulative accounts – prefix 89 or 93
Transient vendor’s licenses – prefix 89, 90, 91, 94, or 99

Example vendor's license number: 99-123456

CustomQuestions.Question: What is your Ohio Customer ID?
CustomQuestions.HelpText: Enter your customer ID.  Example: 123-4567-890

CustomQuestions.Question: Ohio eServices Account Number
CustomQuestions.HelpText: This account number is formatted like UST-1234-567890 and can be found by logging in and reviewing your eServices sales tax account. Not every sales tax account has this format. If your account displays a number formatted like 99-123456, leave this field blank.

StandardQuestions.HelpText:

12-3456789 or TF-3456789
123456789 or TF3456789
StandardQuestions.GRLabel: Taxpayer ID or Employer Identification Number (EIN)

CustomQuestions.Question: Washington Unified Business Identifier (UBI)
CustomQuestions.HelpText:
Find your Washington UBI by checking the documents you received when you registered your business to collect and remit sales and use tax in Washington.
Example UBI: 602-123-456
Washington's MyDOR website will occasionally require us to update an existing password upon login. If prompted, Avalara may update your password in order to continue filing on your behalf. If you are unable to login to your account, please make sure that you check this page for updated login credentials before contacting Avalara Support.

CustomQuestions.Question: Which B&O classification would you like calculated against your Washington nontaxable sales? Please enter 1, 2, 3, 4, or 5 to select your nontaxable B&O tax rate or select 0 if you are fully exempt from paying B&O tax altogether.
CustomQuestions.HelpText:
Please enter 1, 2, 3, 4, or 5 to apply one of the following tax rates to any non-taxable sales you may have during the month:
1. Royalties - Line 10 (1.5%)
2. Wholesale - Line 11 (0.484%)
3. Service and Other Activities (Less Than $1,000,000 in the prior year) - Line 15 (1.5%)
4: Service and Other Activities ($1,000,000 to $4,999,999.99 in the prior year) - Line 16 (1.75%)
5: Service and Other Activities ($5,000,000 or More in the prior year) - 2.1%
If you are exempt from paying B&O tax in Washington state completely, enter 0 here and we will deduct out all B&O tax for Retailing and Wholesaling using the ‘No Local Activity’ deduction.

If you are unsure which classification applies to you, please contact Washington DOR directly to confirm.

If you require multiple selections or the manufacturing with or without the multiple activities tax credit (MATC), please create a case with our Support team.

For more information on B&O tax rates, see Set up Washington returns.

CustomQuestions.Question: Are you required to pay Litter Tax on your Washington Sales Tax Return?
CustomQuestions.HelpText: If you answer "Yes" to this question, Avalara will auto-calculate litter tax using your total Washington sales as the taxable amount. If you wish to use a different taxable amount you must submit that number to us prior to your return’s locking date. For more information on Washington Litter Tax, see the Washington State Department of Revenue website.

CustomQuestions.Question: Washington Excise Letter ID
CustomQuestions.HelpText: Entering your Washington Letter ID will ensure that we can e-file your return if you have not yet added your UBI Excise account to your Washington login. The Letter ID begins with "L" and is found in the upper right-hand corner of any Excise account correspondence from the state. Example Washington Letter ID: L1234567890

CustomQuestions.Question: Have you granted third-party access to Avalara's bulk filing login, AvalaraWA24?
CustomQuestions.HelpText: In order for us to access your Washington UBI Excise Account for filing you need to grant third-party access to Avalara's filing login, AvalaraWA24. for more information, see Add Avalara to your WA state DOR account. Once you have granted third-party access, enter "Yes" into this field to continue.
